summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting recognizing Detective Steve Maynard as 2020 Police Officer of the Year and Sergeant Cody Collier for a Drug Interdiction Award. The board approved multiple training travel requests, a fire/EMS budget true-up saving $46,975, a mutual aid agreement, the police collective bargaining agreement for 2021-2023, demolition of a township-owned vacant house, engineering for the Charles Snyder Road/SR 28 widening project, and appointed several members to the zoning commission and board of zoning appeals. Community members raised concerns about transportation for low-income residents and provided park board updates.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills for Feb 24 - Mar 9, 2021
Approved out-of-town travel for Officer Greg McAllister for death investigation training at $550
Approved out-of-town travel for Sergeant Dylan West for subject control instructor training at $850
Approved fire and EMS budget true-up resulting in $46,975.20 reduction in appropriations
Approved Resolution R005-2021 for fire and EMS mutual aid agreement with Clermont County agencies
Approved Christmas and New Year's holiday office closings for Dec 23-24 and Dec 30-31, 2021
Approved $10,000 increase in general fund appropriations for administration building repairs and maintenance
Authorized Township Administrator to execute contract with Choice One Engineering for Charles Snyder Road/SR 28 widening project at $18,500 plus $3,500 allowance
Approved Community Cleanup Day for Saturday June 5, 2021 from 8am to noon
Authorized Township Administrator to execute contract with Edmonds Landscaping not to exceed $16,000 for demolition of 1910 Main Street
Approved collective bargaining agreement with Goshen Township Police Association for 2021-2023 retroactive to January 1, 2021
Approved increase of police extra duty employment rate to $40 per hour for school district and mobile home park details
Reappointed Lee Lewis to zoning commission for 5-year term (2021-2025)
Appointed David Frisbee as alternate to zoning commission for 2-year term (2021-2022)
Reappointed Ed Sigla to board of zoning appeals for 5-year term (2021-2025)
Appointed Tom Curry to board of zoning appeals to replace Mary Donna Elam for unexpired term (2018-2022)
Appointed Sean Burke to board of zoning appeals for 5-year term (2021-2025)
Appointed Derek Holmes as alternate to board of zoning appeals for 2-year term (2021-2022)
Adjourned meeting at 8:41 p.m.
action_items: Harry (Township Administrator) to return calls and address Don Combs' zoning/property questions
Laura to get mutual aid agreement signed after meeting for submission
Steve (Administrator) to notify employees of Christmas/New Year holiday schedule
Rumpke confirmed for Community Cleanup Day on June 5, 2021
Choice One Engineering to begin design for Charles Snyder Road/SR 28 widening project
Edmonds Landscaping to execute demolition of 1910 Main Street
Chief Rose to invite new officers to future meetings one at a time
Notification to be sent regarding March 23 meeting cancellation and rescheduling to March 30
key_amounts: $46,975.20 reduction in fire and EMS budget appropriations due to CBA true-up
$550 for Officer McAllister death investigation training
$850 for Sergeant West subject control instructor training
$10,000 increase for administration building repairs and maintenance
$18,500 plus $3,500 allowance for Choice One Engineering contract for Charles Snyder Road/SR 28 project
$16,000 not to exceed for demolition of 1910 Main Street by Edmonds Landscaping
$40 per hour new police extra duty rate (increased from $36)
notable_topics: 2020 Police Officer of the Year recognition for Detective Steve Maynard
Drug Interdiction Award for Sergeant Cody Collier
Fire and EMS budget true-up following collective bargaining agreement
Clermont County mutual aid agreement renewal (Resolution R005-2021)
Charles Snyder Road and State Route 28 widening project funded by CDBG grant
Demolition of township-owned vacant house at 1910 Main Street
Community Cleanup Day scheduled for June 5, 2021
Transportation needs for low-income and disabled residents raised by community member
Park board update including Easter egg hunt, pancake breakfast, and Ava's Army special needs playground
Potential transfer of park to Clermont County Park District
Zoning commission and BZA appointments and reappointments
Police extra duty pay rate increase
Holiday schedule for Christmas and New Year's 2021
Four new police officers hired: Charlie Saylor, Kyle Haupt, Tyler Schmidt, Louis Carter
attendees: Detective Steve Maynard (Officer of the Year)
notes: The March 23 meeting was canceled and rescheduled to March 30. A lengthy exchange occurred with community member Don Combs regarding zoning overlay districts and difficulty reaching township staff, which became somewhat contentious. The meeting included an executive session from 7:45 to 8:36 p.m. to discuss employee compensation and union negotiations. The police CBA was initially misstated as retroactive to 2020 but corrected to 2021. Officer Richwine and Officer Davidson were also noted as Drug Interdiction Award recipients but were not present. Part-time firefighter paramedic Donald Books was hired full-time. The park board is exploring transfer of Goshen Community Park to the Clermont County Park District.
